facts.text: Campbell CDP was named after John Campbell, 4th Earl of Loudoun, and once served as a 19th‑century summer resort for Albany’s wealthiest.
Loudon Road, once called Old Plank Road, is lined with several historic Jeffersonian mansions that date back to the early 1800s.
The 1927 clubhouse, modeled on George Washington’s Mount Vernon, was built by local residents who wanted a nearby social venue.
Campbell’s historic district features homes such as the Hughson Mansion and Springwood Manor, all listed on the National Register of Historic Places.
Campbell sits directly north of Albany and south of Newtonville, placing it in the heart of the Capital District.
The original hamlet began at the intersection of Loudon Road and Crumitie Road, where the first plank road crossed.
